---
title: "Generate-Verify-Repair Compression"
chapter_id: "generate-verify-repair-compression"
part_id: "routing-compression-representation-substrates"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-25"
primary_sources:
  - "bbvca_v9"
  - "bbvca_main"
  - "cgs"
  - "rankfold_neuralfold"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Appendix C now records exact passage-reviewed mappings for all assigned Generate-Verify-Repair Compression sources; support remains at argument until a codec, bounded-search harness, reconstruction benchmark, repair-cost benchmark, final-rate experiment, or reproduced artifact-compression result exists."
  - "Only the compression_receipt fixture and narrow finite Lean reconstruction/verification predicates have been implemented; no BBVCA Prototype A implementation, entropy coder, exact reconstruction harness, RankFold/NeuralFold artifact run, or rate comparison has been executed from this repository."
  - "BBVCA main and RankFold/NeuralFold are used as architecture lineage and comparison points only; they do not prove codec correctness, compression efficiency, universality, or working decode behavior here."
---

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `generate-verify-repair-compression` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Representation, and Substrates |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-25 |
| Primary source records | `bbvca_v9`, `bbvca_main`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `bbvca_v9`, `bbvca_main`; supporting: `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold` |
| Source loading state | source notes and passage-reviewed local raw-cache mappings for `bbvca_v9`, `bbvca_main`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`; no raw source text is published |
| Test state | `compression_receipt.valid.json` passes repository-level protocol fixture validation; finite reconstruction/verification predicates implemented in `AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair`; reconstruction-quality, repair-cost, bounded-search, and rate tests remain planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter uses BBVCA v9 as the primary discipline for contract-relative generate-verify-repair compression. It does not claim that this repository contains a working codec, reproduced compression ratios, or a successful bounded-search implementation. The `compression_receipt` fixture validates the public record shape, and the Lean module proves only two finite reconstruction/verification predicates.

Following compact generative systems, this chapter handles the stricter case where compressed generation claims reconstruction. If a generator fails to carry the whole target, the exact remainder must be paid as repair, fallback, or negative rate evidence rather than hidden inside a success story.

## Problem

Some representations may be compressed by storing a lawful generator plus exact repairs, but the book needs disciplined claims around this.

The compression chapters sit where the efficient-ASI thesis becomes concrete. If the system can store a compact generator plus a small exact remainder, it can reuse structure instead of carrying every detail at full cost. But compression rhetoric is dangerous: a compact story is not a compressor, and a generated approximation is not exact reconstruction.

This chapter defines the contract that keeps the idea honest. A compressed artifact must name what is public, what is transmitted, what the generator produces, what verification checked, what repair residuals restore, what the fallback is, and what rate accounting was used. Without those fields, the stack has not compressed anything governable; it has only renamed loss.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Compression narratives can overclaim exactness without bounded search, verification, repair accounting, and fallback criteria.

BBVCA v9 is useful because it hardens the intuition. It rejects the fantasy that an arbitrary larger artifact can be recovered from a smaller private seed alone. The useful form is shared-law compression: a public law family carries repeatable structure, an artifact-specific seed selects local behavior, verification compares generated output to the target, and exact repair streams pay for what the generator missed.

The hardest practical issue is rate honesty. A search-time proxy can guide a dynamic program, but it is not the final encoded length. The source therefore separates proxy scoring from final serialization and requires calibration, anti-pruning margins, and refresh when realized rates diverge.

## Core Claim

[generate-verify-repair-compression.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Generate-verify-repair compression should store the smallest useful lawful explanation plus the cheapest exact remainder when generation fails.

The claim is architectural: it says how this layer should be shaped if the whole system is expected to remain governed under growth. The current support state is `argument`, so the claim should be read as a design rationale unless a later evidence bundle promotes it. The assigned source set - BBVCA_v9_final_public_release, Big Bang Volumetric Compression Architecture, Compact Generative Systems, and RankFold + NeuralFold - gives the chapter its vocabulary and comparison points, but the public manuscript keeps source-derived support separate from the draft itself.

### Claim-source mapping status

Appendix C now carries passage-reviewed mappings for all four assigned sources. The mappings support reconstruction contracts, shared-law generation, exact repair streams, bounded verification, rate honesty, residual accounting, and artifact-compression comparison points, not a working compressor, BBVCA Prototype A implementation, entropy-coder run, RankFold/NeuralFold artifact run, or reproduced compression ratio.

| Source | What it supports | Limit |
|---|---|---|
| `bbvca_v9` | Reconstruction contracts, shared law families, exact repair streams, bounded local verification, explicit fallback, boundary/interface costs, proxy-rate versus final serialization discipline, and anti-universality constraints. | BBVCA Prototype A, compression ratios, codec correctness, and rate experiments have not been implemented or run here. |
| `bbvca_main` | Hierarchical causal/volumetric reconstruction, compact generator states, deterministic laws, multiscale verification, side information, and repair risks as lineage for the stricter v9 formulation. | Earlier architecture draft only; use v9 for final bounded rate/exactness claims, and no local codec or benchmark exists. |
| `cgs` | Compact seeds, rule systems, residual/error channels, verification contracts, governance interfaces, and hidden complexity debt around compressed generation. | Does not establish that any generate-verify-repair codec preserves utility, improves rate, or reconstructs data in this repo. |
| `rankfold_neuralfold` | Low-rank residual coding, artifact-to-tensor conversion, WORM archive assumptions, manifests, codec parameters, deterministic decode, and reconstruction checks as comparison points. | Architecture and implementation-plan source only; no local compression benchmark, implementation artifact, deterministic decoder, or reproduced ratio is present. |

## Mechanism

Generate-verify-repair is compact generation with an exactness bill attached. BBVCA v9 supplies the key discipline: define the reconstruction contract, generate what lawful structure can honestly produce, verify local regions, and store exact repair residuals for every mismatch. CGS explains why the residual channel is not optional, and RankFold/NeuralFold supplies the artifact-archive comparison point where codec parameters, manifests, and deterministic checks have to travel with the compressed object.

A Compression Receipt is the exactness ledger for that process. It should say what was generated by law, what was repaired literally, which verification passed, when literal fallback was cheaper, which boundary/interface costs were counted, and which rate numbers are proxy estimates rather than final serialized costs.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Reconstruction contract"] --> B["Public law family"]
  A --> C["Artifact-specific seed"]
  B --> D["Generate candidate"]
  C --> D
  D --> E{"Local verification"}
  E -- "match" --> F["Accept generated region"]
  E -- "mismatch" --> G["Exact repair residual"]
  F --> H["Rate accounting"]
  G --> H
  H --> I{"Cheaper than literal?"}
  I -- "yes" --> J["Final serialized stream"]
  I -- "no" --> K["Literal fallback / negative result"]
  J --> L["Decode: replay generator + repairs"]
  K --> L
```

The process is contract-relative: a public law family and artifact-specific seed generate candidate regions; verification compares those regions to the declared target; mismatches become exact repair residuals or literal fallback; and rate accounting includes law IDs, seeds, interfaces, repairs, metadata, and proxy/final-rate gaps. Exactness is a property of the full receipt, not the generator alone.

The compression record should include the reconstruction contract, law-family id, seed payload, verification region, repair stream, literal fallback, interface costs, search bound, proxy model, final entropy model, and proxy gap. That record is the bridge from a beautiful compression idea to an auditable ASI substrate.

## Interfaces

The interface is a Compression Receipt.

- Evidence measures reconstruction and utility.
- Routing chooses compressed or full representation.
- VCM uses summaries with loss contracts.

Minimum fields:

- `artifact_id`
- `reconstruction_contract`
- `public_law_family`
- `seed`
- `generated_regions`
- `verification_result`
- `repair_residual`
- `fallback_threshold`
- `rate_accounting`
- `non_claims`

VCM can consume lossy summaries only when loss is declared. Routing can choose a full artifact instead of a compressed one when repair cost or verification failure crosses a threshold. Evidence ledgers can reject exactness claims when the receipt lacks verification or residual accounting.

## Invariants

The compression invariants keep compactness honest: every reconstruction, repair stream, declared loss, and fallback threshold must remain visible when a shorter artifact is routed into later layers.

- Verification precedes exactness claims.
- Repair cost is counted.
- Search bounds are explicit.

The exactness invariant is the important one: decoded generator output plus exact repair must equal the target under the stated contract. If the contract permits loss, the loss must be named and cannot support a lossless claim. If the repair stream is larger than the original, the system should record a negative result, not hide the failed compression behind a successful decode.

## Failure modes

The failure model is part of the design, not an afterthought. This layer is expected to fail in ways that can be detected, quarantined, and used to improve the architecture.

- Unbounded search.
- Verification skipped.
- Repair larger than original data.

The most common failure is side-information laundering: moving data into law IDs, parameters, boundaries, or repair streams and then celebrating the seed as small. A second failure is proxy dishonesty: optimizing a cheap proxy objective and never checking the final serialized rate. A third is domain mismatch: forcing volumetric or lawful structure onto data that does not have enough exploitable locality.

## Minimal implementation

A compression record with generator, verification result, repair residual, and fallback threshold.

The smallest useful implementation is not a new universal codec. It is a tiny receipt schema and a toy deterministic generator over small arrays or text blocks. The test should show three cases: generated output matches directly, generated output requires an exact repair stream, and repair/fallback makes the compressed form larger than the literal baseline.

The current fixture covers only the receipt side of that implementation: reconstruction contract, public law family, seed, generated regions, verification result, repair residual, fallback threshold, rate accounting, and non-claims. A prototype claim remains unpromoted until code, command, environment, corpus, and result are recorded.

`AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair` adds a bounded reconstruction predicate requiring an exact claim to satisfy `generator output + repair residual = target`, plus a bounded failed-verification predicate blocking exactness promotion. These proofs do not implement a codec, verifier, search procedure, repair stream, rate accounting experiment, or corpus result.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Compression receipt fixture validation | Validate that a receipt records reconstruction contract, law family, seed, generated regions, verification result, repair residual, fallback threshold, rate accounting, and non-claims. | implemented; passing via `python3 scripts/validate_protocol_examples.py` |
| Exact reconstruction predicate | Check that an exact claim requires generator output plus repair residual to equal the target in a finite record. | implemented in `AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair`; checked by Lean build |
| Failed verification promotion predicate | Check that failed verification blocks exactness promotion. | implemented in `AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair`; checked by Lean build |
| Reconstruction quality test | Run a generator and verifier against a declared target and check exact reconstruction when the receipt claims exactness; lossy contracts must name the loss. | planned; not run |
| Repair-cost accounting test | Compare seed, law ID, repair residual, and metadata against a literal baseline and record a negative result when the repaired form is larger. | planned; not run |
| Bounded-search failure test | Force the generator past its declared search budget and check that the result falls back, quarantines, or stays unpromoted. | planned; not run |

The implemented row validates fixture/schema consistency only. The remaining rows require a real generator, verifier, repair stream, fallback criterion, and rate comparison; they are not reported results. When a codec test is implemented, the chapter should link to the command, fixture, environment notes, corpus, and result summary, and Appendix E should be regenerated or updated accordingly.

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:compression.gvr.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair` | An exact reconstruction claim requires generator output plus repair residual to equal the target. | implemented |
| `lean:compression.gvr.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair` | A failed verification blocks exactness promotion. | implemented |

These Lean hooks are implemented as finite-record predicates. The receipt schema and Lean predicates do not prove equality between decoded output and real target data, codec correctness, or compression efficiency.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bbvca_v9` | BBVCA_v9_final_public_release | compression_representation | Prefer v9. Generate-verify-repair compression from seeded local laws, bounded search, two-phase rate discipline. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `bbvca_main` | Big Bang Volumetric Compression Architecture | compression_representation | Earlier/main BBVCA family doc. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | compression_representation | Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `rankfold_neuralfold` | RankFold + NeuralFold | compression_representation | Tensor/artifact compression. Low-rank residual coding plus functional preprocessing and probe-route fallback. | source note available; local raw cache available |

The crosswalk is intentionally version-sensitive. `bbvca_v9` is the preferred source for final generate-verify-repair wording because it names proxy-rate discipline, repair streams, bounded local verification, and anti-universality constraints. `bbvca_main` remains lineage. `cgs` and `rankfold_neuralfold` connect the same residual-honesty idea to compact systems and artifact compression. None of these assignments is a local compression result.

## Summary

Generate-Verify-Repair Compression defines one boundary in the ASI Stack: Generate-verify-repair compression should store the smallest useful lawful explanation plus the cheapest exact remainder when generation fails. Its job is to make a capability more governable, not merely more impressive.

The chapter's discipline is residual honesty. A generator is useful only when the stack pays for what it fails to generate, checks the result against a declared reconstruction contract, and records when literal storage was better. The next chapter moves that same accounting from stored artifacts to time: faster generation is useful only after verification, fallback, and accepted-output costs are counted.

In other words, a faster generator inherits the same burden as a smaller representation: proposed output is cheap only if the verifier can afford to decide what should be accepted.
